 {"id":"rust_agent_network_observer-lr2","title":"Alert output formatting and logging","description":"Deliverables:
- Alert output to stderr with distinct formatting:
  [ALERT] 2026-01-20T10:00:00Z | domain-watch | api.unknown.com matched '*unknown*'
  [ALERT] 2026-01-20T10:00:01Z | max-connections | 101 connections (threshold: 100)
  [ALERT] 2026-01-20T10:00:02Z | long-duration | connection to api.anthropic.com:443 lasted 45000ms

- Colors:
  - Alert prefix: bright red background
  - Alert type: yellow
  - Details: white

- Bell: if --alert-bell, output \a after alert line

- JSON mode: alerts as JSON objects on stderr with 'alert': true field

- SQLite: add 'alert' boolean column to events table
  - Alerts logged with alert=true for later querying

- Summary: include alert count in final summary
  'Alerts triggered: 5 (3 domain-watch, 2 max-connections)'","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:00:54.170178182Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T10:25:35.010307656Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T10:25:35.010262661Z","close_reason":"done","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-lr2","depends_on_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-2iu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:00:54.200230439Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-lr2","depends_on_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-20u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:01:10.488350819Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-lr2","depends_on_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-291","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:01:10.788894643Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"rust_agent_network_observer-n2d","title":"Config validation command","description":"Purpose:
- Catch configuration errors before runtime
- Reduce debugging time for 'why isn't my config working'
- Provide clear feedback on config file issues

User impact:
- 'rano config check' validates all config files and reports issues
- 'rano config show' displays resolved configuration
- Clear error messages point to exact file:line of problems

Features:
- rano config check: validate config.conf, rano.toml, presets
- rano config show: display resolved config as key=value
- rano config show --json: display as JSON for automation
- rano config paths: show where config files are loaded from

Validation checks:
- Syntax: valid key=value, valid TOML
- Known keys: warn on unknown keys (typo detection)
- Value types: numbers parse, booleans valid
- Patterns: regex/glob patterns compile
- Paths: referenced files exist (log_dir, sqlite)
- Provider config: patterns are valid, mode is merge/replace

Exit codes:
- 0: valid configuration
- 1: errors found (won't run)
- 2: warnings only (will run but may have issues)

Testing:
- Unit tests for each validation check
- E2E script with invalid configs to verify error messages","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:06:13.5648513Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T22:27:38.30557649Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T22:27:38.305506198Z","close_reason":"done","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-n2d","depends_on_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-3o1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:06:13.59425301Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"rust_agent_network_observer-oiy","title":"Add E2E coverage for top-level help command discoverability","description":"Add an E2E script that runs rano --help and asserts the top-level usage/command list includes report/export/diff/status/config/update. This complements unit tests by validating user-visible CLI output.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-13T11:38:12.348639736-06:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-13T11:51:59.931177135-06:00","closed_at":"2026-02-13T11:51:59.931195308-06:00","close_reason":"Added and passed E2E help discoverability test"}

 {"id":"rust_agent_network_observer-q9u","title":"Automatic session naming","description":"Purpose:
- Generate human-readable session names instead of opaque run_ids
- Enable 'remember that session last Tuesday' conversations

User impact:
- Sessions named like 'morning-claude-audit-2026-01-20' instead of '12345-1705512345000'
- 'rano report --session morning-claude-audit-2026-01-20' for lookup
- Easier to discuss sessions in team contexts

Features:
- Auto-generate name on session start: {time_of_day}-{primary_provider}-{pattern}-{date}
- --session-name <name> to override auto-generated name
- Store in sessions table alongside run_id
- Report and export accept session name as filter

Time of day mapping:
- 00:00-06:00: night
- 06:00-12:00: morning
- 12:00-18:00: afternoon
- 18:00-24:00: evening

Testing:
- Unit tests for name generation
- E2E script verifying names appear in report
- Test custom --session-name override","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:07:05.697380819Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T03:31:23.908029005Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T03:31:23.907986235Z","close_reason":"done","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-q9u","depends_on_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-3o1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:07:05.727782292Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"rust_agent_network_observer-uj6","title":"Export tests and documentation","description":"Deliverables:
- Unit tests:
  - test_csv_escape_quotes() - 'foo"bar' -> '"foo""bar"'
  - test_csv_escape_comma() - 'foo,bar' wrapped in quotes
  - test_csv_escape_newline() - embedded newlines wrapped
  - test_csv_header_generation() - correct field order
  - test_jsonl_valid_json() - each line parses as JSON
  - test_export_empty_result() - no crash on empty data

- E2E test script (tests/e2e/export-formats.sh):
  - Seed SQLite with test data
  - Export to CSV, validate header and row count
  - Export to JSONL, validate each line is valid JSON
  - Test --since/--until filters
  - Test --provider filter
  - Test --domain filter
  - Roundtrip: export CSV, import to new SQLite, compare counts
  - Log: filter inputs, row counts, sample output

- Documentation:
  - README section: 'Exporting Data'
  - Example: 'rano export --format csv --since 24h > daily.csv'
  - Example: 'rano export --format jsonl | jq -r .domain | sort | uniq -c'
  - Field reference table","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:01:52.683237477Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T10:58:11.050454256Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T10:58:11.050411055Z","close_reason":"done","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-uj6","depends_on_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-1vz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:01:52.694725221Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-uj6","depends_on_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-1gl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:01:58.073466211Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"rust_agent_network_observer-vb7","title":"Implement ancestry chain reading","description":"Deliverables:
- read_ancestry(pid) -> Vec<(u32, String)>:
  - Walk /proc/{pid}/stat to get PPID
  - Recursively read parent until PPID=0 or PPID=1
  - Return chain from root to current: [(init_pid, init_comm), ..., (pid, comm)]

- AncestryCache struct:
  - cache: HashMap<u32, (Vec<(u32, String)>, Instant)>
  - ttl: Duration (default 30s)
  - get_or_compute(pid) method with staleness check

- Staleness detection:
  - If cached entry exists, verify current comm matches cached comm
  - If mismatch (PID recycled), invalidate and recompute

- format_ancestry(chain) -> String:
  - Output: 'zsh(123) → tmux(456) → claude(789)'
  - Truncate if >5 levels: '... → parent(456) → claude(789)'
  - Handle single-level: 'claude(789)'

- Integration:
  - Add ancestry_cache to main state
  - Lookup ancestry when --show-ancestry enabled
  - Pass ancestry to event formatting","design":"Implementation approach:
- Reuse existing read_ppid() from descendant tracking
- Stack-based iteration (not recursion) to avoid stack overflow
- Stop at PPID=0 (kernel) or PPID=1 (init/systemd)
- Reverse to get root→leaf order

Performance considerations:
- Cache is essential - ancestry changes rarely
- 30s TTL is reasonable (processes don't typically reparent)
- Staleness check: comm is sufficient (PID reuse with same comm is rare)
- Memory: ~100 bytes per cached entry, max ~1000 tracked PIDs = 100KB

Edge cases:
- Process exits during walk: return partial chain with '?' for missing
- Zombie process: use comm from cmdline fallback
- Init containers: stop at container init, not host init","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:02:49.224824805Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T09:11:08.313186516Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T09:11:08.313142694Z","close_reason":"done","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-vb7","depends_on_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-1ju","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:02:49.294596381Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":4,"issue_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-vb7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented ancestry chain reading + cache in src/main.rs (read_ancestry, AncestryCache w/ TTL + PID reuse check, format_ancestry truncation). Integrated optional ancestry output into connect/close event formatting (pretty + JSON) gated by args.show_ancestry. Added show_ancestry field in MonitorArgs. cargo test -q passed.","created_at":"2026-01-21T09:10:58Z"}]}
